description_long: This gatehouse is a fortified stone structure surrounded by a rectangular perimeter wall with six cylindrical corner towers, each topped with battlements. The main building features a steeply pitched gable roof made of dark slate, and its facade is marked by a prominent arched entryway flanked by two smaller turrets. The entry is further accentuated by a projecting porch with a secondary arch and a small balcony above. The walls and towers are constructed from rough-hewn stone, giving the structure a robust, medieval appearance. Small slit windows are visible on the towers and the main building, providing limited visibility and light. The entire complex sits on a green base, suggesting a grassy or landscaped setting.
description: A fortified stone gatehouse with a steep gable roof, arched entryway, and six cylindrical corner towers, all enclosed by a battlemented wall on a grassy base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entryway, projecting porch, and two flanking turrets, with the full perimeter wall and corner towers visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the main building, the steep gable roof, and the alignment of the wall and towers; entryway is not visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main building and the symmetry of the wall and towers, with no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gable end of the building, the wall, and towers, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone doorway with a projecting porch and small balcony above
attribute_window_placement: Small slit windows on towers and main building, asymmetrically placed
